note: These define expected components for each page archetype. 'Core' components are expected on any listed company site. 'Enhanced' components are expected for FTSE 100 or equivalent. The reasoner checks evidence against these and reports present/absent. It does not say 'best practice says' — it says 'expected component not found'.

homepage.label: Corporate homepage
homepage.description: The front door. Should orient any visitor — investor, customer, jobseeker, journalist — within seconds.

core.check: Can a first-time visitor understand what the company does within one sentence?

core.component: Primary navigation to all major sections
core.check: Links to About, Products/Services, Investors, Sustainability, Careers, News, Contact
Some indication of which sectors or markets the company operates in
Recent content visible — press releases, stories, or announcements
Path to contact page or direct contact visible

enhanced.component: Share price or investor quick link
enhanced.check: Share price ticker, widget, or prominent IR link
Company purpose, vision, or strategic narrative above the fold
Latest results headline or key financial metric visible
ESG or sustainability message visible on homepage
Job or careers link visible beyond just navigation

about.label: About / at a glance
about.description: The company's self-portrait. Should establish identity, scale, purpose, and structure.

core.check: What the company does, in its own words
Divisions, segments, or business units identified
Where the company operates — countries, regions, or key locations
Revenue, employee count, customer count, or other size metrics
Routes to strategy, leadership, history, or similar

enhanced.check: Explicit purpose, mission, or vision statement
Revenue, profit, or growth metric visible
Major brands, products, or services highlighted
Timeline, founding story, or heritage narrative

strategy.label: Strategy page
strategy.description: How the company intends to create value. Should connect purpose to priorities to measurable targets.

core.check: Named strategic priorities, pillars, or themes
Description of the market environment or trends driving strategy
How the strategy creates value for stakeholders

enhanced.check: Quantified mid-term or long-term targets with timeframes
How the company makes money — revenue model, competitive position
Key performance indicators linked to strategy
Evidence of progress against stated strategy — not just aspirational
Cross-reference to CMD, annual report, or investor presentation for detail

leadership.label: Leadership and governance
leadership.description: Who runs the company and how it is governed. Should establish credibility and accountability.

core.check: Board members listed with names and roles
Senior management listed — CEO, CFO, and direct reports
Background, experience, or career summary for each person
Board committees identified — audit, remuneration, nomination at minimum

enhanced.check: Professional photographs of board and executive team
Independence status, appointment dates, or tenure indicated
Gender, ethnic, or skills diversity data visible
Reference to UK Corporate Governance Code or equivalent
Committee remits or links to terms of reference documents

ir_landing.label: Investor relations landing page
ir_landing.description: The investor's front door. Should provide immediate orientation and fast access to the most-needed content.

core.check: Link to latest results, annual report, and presentations
Share price display, chart, or link to share price page
Upcoming dates — results, AGM, ex-dividend
IR team contact or registrar details
RNS feed, company announcements, or regulatory disclosure link

enhanced.check: Key financial metrics from most recent results visible without clicking through
Summary of why to invest — positioning, competitive advantages, growth drivers
Next reporting date, AGM, or investor event prominently displayed
Fast access to results, reports, governance, shareholder info, contact
Company guidance visible or link to consensus data
Major shareholders, free float, or share capital information
Credit ratings, bond information, or debt investor section
Sign-up for IR alerts, results notifications, or news alerts

results.label: Results and financial reporting
results.description: Where investors find performance data. Should provide immediate access to the latest numbers and historical context.

core.check: Most recent results press release or RNS accessible
Analyst presentation or slides for latest results
Revenue, profit, EPS, dividend, and cash flow visible or in linked document
Current year annual report available as PDF

enhanced.check: Results presentation webcast or replay available
Prior year results accessible — at least 3 years
Charts, data explorer, or interactive financial data tool
Performance by division, segment, or geography
Forward-looking guidance for next period visible

annual_report.label: Annual report landing page
annual_report.description: Access point for the company's primary disclosure document.

core.check: Full annual report downloadable as PDF
Which financial year the report covers is unambiguous

enhanced.check: Individual sections available — strategic report, governance, financials separately
Online interactive version of the annual report
Prior years' annual reports accessible
ESEF-tagged version available (regulatory requirement for EU/UK listed)

sustainability.label: Sustainability / ESG
sustainability.description: The company's environmental, social, and governance narrative. Should demonstrate commitment with evidence, not just aspiration.

core.check: Climate, emissions, or environmental impact addressed
People, communities, health and safety, or human rights addressed
Business ethics, compliance, or governance standards addressed
Quantified sustainability targets — net zero date, emissions targets, or similar

enhanced.check: Downloadable sustainability or ESG report separate from annual report
Reference to TCFD, GRI, SASB, UN SDGs, or equivalent frameworks
Quantified sustainability metrics — emissions data, safety rates, diversity stats
Materiality matrix or description of material ESG topics
Supplier standards, modern slavery, or supply chain sustainability addressed
Third-party assurance or verification of sustainability data

careers.label: Careers and employer brand
careers.description: The employer's front door. Should attract talent with a clear proposition, not just list vacancies.

core.check: Route to current job listings — on-site search or link to careers platform
Some content about what it's like to work here — beyond just 'join us'

enhanced.check: Explicit EVP — what the company offers employees beyond salary
Company culture, behaviours, or working environment described
Key benefits, development opportunities, or perks listed
Real employee voices — profiles, quotes, videos, or day-in-the-life content
Apprenticeship, internship, or graduate programme content
D&I commitment, employee networks, or inclusion narrative
Where the company has offices or sites — relevant for candidates

products.label: Products and services
products.description: What the company sells. Should make the offer clear to customers, prospects, and investors alike.

core.check: Products or services listed and described
Products organised logically — by division, market, customer type, or use case
At least some products have their own detail pages

enhanced.check: Products contextualised by who uses them or what problem they solve
Evidence of products in use — customer examples, deployments, or outcomes
Performance data, specifications, or technical detail for key products
Aftermarket, support, or service offerings described
Connection to future products, R&D pipeline, or technology roadmap

news.label: News and media centre
news.description: The corporate newsroom. Should serve journalists, analysts, and anyone tracking the company.

core.check: Corporate press releases accessible — current and archived
RNS or regulatory news feed for listed companies
Press office contact details

enhanced.check: News organised by type — corporate, trade, stories, regulatory
Email alerts, RSS, or news subscription option
Image library, brand assets, logos, or media kit
Stories, thought leadership, or editorial content beyond press releases
Links to corporate social media channels

contact.label: Contact page
contact.description: The universal fallback. Should route any enquiry to the right place efficiently.

core.check: Phone number, email, or contact form for general enquiries
Company registered address visible (regulatory requirement for listed companies)

enhanced.check: Different contact routes for different enquiry types — investors, media, customers, careers
Key office or site addresses listed
Ethics line, whistleblowing contact, or speak-up mechanism
